Original question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Draft answer:
FINAL_ANSWER: Use a Trie to find the shortest unique extensions.
JUSTIFICATION: Trie traversal identifies minimal bits needed for unique prefixes.

Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Insert all IDs into a Trie, then for each ID, find the shortest path to a leaf or a node with a unique prefix.
JUSTIFICATION: Trie traversal finds minimal bits to ensure unique identification.
